english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Ephesians

verses.latin_vulgate: Filii, obedite parentibus vestris in Domino: hoc enim justum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ τέκνα ὑπακούετε τοῖς γονεῦσιν ὑμῶν ἐν κυρίῳ τοῦτο γάρ ἐστιν δίκαιον
verses.king_james: Children, obey your parents in the Lord: for this is right.

verses.latin_vulgate: Honora patrem tuum, et matrem tuam, quod est mandatum primum in promissione:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίμα τὸν πατέρα σου καὶ τὴν μητέρα ἥτις ἐστὶν ἐντολὴ πρώτη ἐν ἐπαγγελίᾳ
verses.king_james: Honour thy father and mother; (which is the first commandment with promise;)

verses.latin_vulgate: ut bene sit tibi, et sis longævus super terram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα εὖ σοι γένηται καὶ ἔσῃ μακροχρόνιος ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vos patres, nolite ad iracundiam provocare filios vestros: sed educate illos in disciplina et correptione Domini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ πατέρες μὴ παροργίζετε τὰ τέκνα ὑμῶν ἀλλ᾽ ἐκτρέφετε αὐτὰ ἐν παιδείᾳ καὶ νουθεσίᾳ κυρίου
verses.king_james: And, ye fathers, provoke not your children to wrath: but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servi, obedite dominis carnalibus cum timore et tremore, in simplicitate cordis vestri, sicut Christo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δοῦλοι ὑπακούετε τοῖς κυρίοις κατὰ σάρκα μετὰ φόβου καὶ τρόμου ἐν ἁπλότητι τῆς καρδίας ὑμῶν ὡς τῷ χριστῷ
verses.king_james: Servants, be obedient to them that are *your* masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as unto Christ;

verses.latin_vulgate: non ad oculum servientes, quasi hominibus placentes, sed ut servi Christi, facientes voluntatem Dei ex animo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ κατ᾽ ὀφθαλμοδουλείαν ὡς ἀνθρωπάρεσκοι ἀλλ᾽ ὡς δοῦλοι τοῦ χριστοῦ ποιοῦντες τὸ θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ ἐκ ψυχῆς
verses.king_james: Not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but as the servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart;

verses.latin_vulgate: cum bona voluntate servientes, sicut Domino, et non hominibus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετ᾽ εὐνοίας δουλεύοντες τῷ κυρίῳ καὶ οὐκ ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: With good will doing service, as to the Lord, and not to men:

verses.latin_vulgate: scientes quoniam unusquisque quodcumque fecerit bonum, hoc recipiet a Domino, sive servus, sive liber.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰδότες ὅτι ὃ ἐάν τι ἕκαστος ποιήσῃ ἀγαθόν τοῦτο κομιεῖται παρὰ τοῦ κυρίου εἴτε δοῦλος εἴτε ἐλεύθερος
verses.king_james: Knowing that whatsoever good thing any man doeth, the same shall he receive of the Lord, whether *he be* bond or free.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vos domini, eadem facite illis, remittentes minas: scientes quia et illorum et vester Dominus est in cælis: et ersonarum acceptio non est apud eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ κύριοι τὰ αὐτὰ ποιεῖτε πρὸς αὐτούς ἀνιέντες τὴν ἀπειλήν εἰδότες ὅτι καὶ ὑμῶν αὐτῶν ὁ κύριός ἐστιν ἐν οὐρανοῖς καὶ προσωποληψία οὐκ ἔστιν παρ᾽ αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And, ye masters, do the same things unto them, forbearing threatening: knowing that your Master also is in heaven; neither is there respect of persons with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: De cetero, fratres, confortamini in Domino, et in potentia virtutis ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ λοιπὸν ἀδελφοί μου ἐνδυναμοῦσθε ἐν κυρίῳ καὶ ἐν τῷ κράτει τῆς ἰσχύος αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might.

verses.latin_vulgate: Induite vos armaturam Dei, ut possitis stare adversus insidias diaboli:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐνδύσασθε τὴν πανοπλίαν τοῦ θεοῦ πρὸς τὸ δύνασθαι ὑμᾶς στῆναι πρὸς τὰς μεθοδείας τοῦ διαβόλου
verses.king_james: Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam non est nobis colluctatio adversus carnem et sanguinem, sed adversus principes, et potestates, adversus mundi rectores tenebrarum harum, contra spiritualia nequitiæ, in cælestibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι οὐκ ἔστιν ἡμῖν ἡ πάλη πρὸς αἷμα καὶ σάρκα ἀλλὰ πρὸς τὰς ἀρχάς πρὸς τὰς ἐξουσίας πρὸς τοὺς κοσμοκράτορας τοῦ σκότους τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου πρὸς τὰ πνευματικὰ τῆς πονηρίας ἐν τοῖς ἐπουρανίοις
verses.king_james: For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high *places*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea accipite armaturam Dei, ut possitis resistere in die malo, et in omnibus perfecti stare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο ἀναλάβετε τὴν πανοπλίαν τοῦ θεοῦ ἵνα δυνηθῆτε ἀντιστῆναι ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ πονηρᾷ καὶ ἅπαντα κατεργασάμενοι στῆναι
verses.king_james: Wherefore take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.

verses.latin_vulgate: State ergo succincti lumbos vestros in veritate, et induti loricam justitiæ,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: στῆτε οὖν περιζωσάμενοι τὴν ὀσφὺν ὑμῶν ἐν ἀληθείᾳ καὶ ἐνδυσάμενοι τὸν θώρακα τῆς δικαιοσύνης
verses.king_james: Stand therefore, having your loins girt about with truth, and having on the breastplate of righteousness;

verses.latin_vulgate: et calceati pedes in præparatione Evangelii pacis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑποδησάμενοι τοὺς πόδας ἐν ἑτοιμασίᾳ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου τῆς εἰρήνης
verses.king_james: And your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace;

verses.latin_vulgate: in omnibus sumentes scutum fidei, in quo possitis omnia tela nequissimi ignea extinguere:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπὶ πᾶσιν ἀναλαβόντες τὸν θυρεὸν τῆς πίστεως ἐν ᾧ δυνήσεσθε πάντα τὰ βέλη τοῦ πονηροῦ τὰ πεπυρωμένα σβέσαι
verses.king_james: Above all, taking the shield of faith, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked.

verses.latin_vulgate: et galeam salutis assumite, et gladium spiritus (quod est verbum Dei),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὴν περικεφαλαίαν τοῦ σωτηρίου δέξασθε καὶ τὴν μάχαιραν τοῦ πνεύματος ὅ ἐστιν ῥῆμα θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God:

verses.latin_vulgate: per omnem orationem et obsecrationem orantes omni tempore in spiritu: et in ipso vigilantes in omni instantia et obsecratione pro omnibus sanctis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ πάσης προσευχῆς καὶ δεήσεως προσευχόμενοι ἐν παντὶ καιρῷ ἐν πνεύματι καὶ εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο ἀγρυπνοῦντες ἐν πάσῃ προσκαρτερήσει καὶ δεήσει περὶ πάντων τῶν ἁγίων
verses.king_james: Praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and watching thereunto with all perseverance and supplication for all saints;

verses.latin_vulgate: et pro me, ut detur mihi sermo in apertione oris mei cum fiducia, notum facere mysterium Evangelii:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑπὲρ ἐμοῦ ἵνα μοι δοθείη λόγος ἐν ἀνοίξει τοῦ στόματός μου ἐν παῤῥησίᾳ γνωρίσαι τὸ μυστήριον τοῦ εὐαγγελίου
verses.king_james: And for me, that utterance may be given unto me, that I may open my mouth boldly, to make known the mystery of the gospel,

verses.latin_vulgate: pro quo legatione fungor in catena, ita ut in ipso audeam, prout oportet me loqui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπὲρ οὗ πρεσβεύω ἐν ἁλύσει ἵνα ἐν αὐτῷ παῤῥησιάσωμαι ὡς δεῖ με λαλῆσαι
verses.king_james: For which I am an ambassador in bonds: that therein I may speak boldly, as I ought to speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut autem et vos sciatis quæ circa me sunt, quid agam, omnia vobis nota faciet Tychicus, carissimus frater, et fidelis minister in Domino:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα δὲ εἰδῆτε καὶ ὑμεῖς τὰ κατ᾽ ἐμέ τί πράσσω πάντα ὑμῖν γνωρίσει τυχικὸς ὁ ἀγαπητὸς ἀδελφὸς καὶ πιστὸς διάκονος ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: But that ye also may know my affairs, *and* how I do, Tychicus, a beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, shall make known to you all things:

verses.latin_vulgate: quem misi ad vos in hoc ipsum, ut cognoscatis quæ circa nos sunt, et consoletur corda vestra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν ἔπεμψα πρὸς ὑμᾶς εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο ἵνα γνῶτε τὰ περὶ ἡμῶν καὶ παρακαλέσῃ τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that ye might know our affairs, and *that* he might comfort your hearts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pax fratribus, et caritas cum fide a Deo Patre et Domino Jesu Christo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰρήνη τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς καὶ ἀγάπη μετὰ πίστεως ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς καὶ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Peace *be* to the brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia cum omnibus qui diligunt Dominum nostrum Jesum Christum in incorruptione. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ χάρις μετὰ πάντων τῶν ἀγαπώντων τὸν κύριον ἡμῶν ἰησοῦν χριστὸν ἐν ἀφθαρσίᾳ ἀμήν
verses.king_james: Grace *be* with all them that love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.
